evals.query: Add entry/exit logging to the getUser(String id) method of my Kora UserService so I can see arguments and the returned value.
expected_behavior: Annotates getUser with @Log from ru.tinkoff.kora.logging.common.annotation
Notes the enclosing class must be non-final (Java) or open (Kotlin) for the aspect to weave
Mentions arguments and result appear only when the class logger is at DEBUG/TRACE, while INFO shows boundary markers > and <
Does not add a manual SLF4J logger field or use field injection

evals.query: How do I enable Kora method logging from scratch in a new service? What dependency and module do I need?
expected_behavior: Adds ru.tinkoff.kora:logging-logback (or logging-common) via the kora-parent BOM without pinning an individual version
Includes the mandatory annotation processor: annotationProcessor "ru.tinkoff.kora:annotation-processors" for Java or ksp "ru.tinkoff.kora:symbol-processors" for Kotlin
Extends LoggingModule on the @KoraApp interface
Does not pin individual ru.tinkoff.kora artifact versions or omit the annotation processor

evals.query: I want every log line in processOrder to include the orderId from the method parameter.
expected_behavior: Adds @Mdc(key = "orderId") to the orderId parameter (or bare @Mdc to use the parameter name as the key)
Imports ru.tinkoff.kora.logging.common.annotation.Mdc, not org.slf4j.MDC
Keeps @Log on the method so the records are emitted
Does not call SLF4J MDC.put manually or use field injection

evals.query: authenticate(username, password, refreshToken) should log the username but never the password or refresh token.
expected_behavior: Annotates the method with @Log
Adds @Log.off to the password and refreshToken parameters so they are excluded from the log line
Optionally adds @Mdc to username for context
Does not log secrets via toString or a manual logger statement

evals.query: Set the @Log level on debugOperation to DEBUG and completely turn off logging for internalCleanup.
expected_behavior: Uses @Log(Level.DEBUG) passing the level as the annotation value attribute (not level =) with Level from org.slf4j.event.Level
Uses @Log.off on internalCleanup to disable logging
States there is no Level.OFF in org.slf4j.event.Level
Does not invent a @Log(level = Level.OFF) attribute

evals.query: I need one method to log only its entry with arguments, and another to log only its exit with the return value.
expected_behavior: Uses @Log.in for the entry-only method
Uses @Log.out for the exit-only method
Notes both default to INFO level and emit only the > or < record respectively
Does not write a manual logger statement or a hand-rolled around-advice wrapper

evals.query: On createOrder add MDC keys for the tenant taken from the tenantId parameter and a generated requestId UUID.
expected_behavior: Adds method-level @Mdc(key = "tenant", value = "${tenantId}") referencing the parameter by name
Adds @Mdc(key = "requestId", value = "${java.util.UUID.randomUUID().toString()}") using expression interpolation
Relies on @Mdc being repeatable so both annotations apply
Does not manually call SLF4J MDC.put in the method body

evals.query: Set a tenant value in MDC at the start of request handling so it persists across the rest of the call on this thread, and explain cleanup.
expected_behavior: Uses @Mdc(key = "tenant", value = "${tenantId}", global = true) so the key persists after the method returns
Warns that global keys leak on pooled threads and must be removed with the static MDC.remove("tenant")
States Kora's MDC has no clear() method, so keys are removed individually
Does not claim MDC.wrap or MDC.clear exist in Kora

evals.query: My upload(fileName, byte[] data, fileSize) method logs the whole file bytes and floods the logs. How do I fix it while keeping useful context?
expected_behavior: Adds @Log.off to the byte[] data parameter so the payload is never rendered or logged
Keeps fileName and fileSize visible, e.g. via @Mdc keys
Recommends running the class logger at INFO in production and KoraAsyncAppender for async output
Does not suggest Logback's stock AsyncAppender, which drops Kora MDC and structured args
